title: bouvardia chrysantha
intro: Bouvardia chrysantha, also known as the golden bouvardia or desert marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve muscle spasms, pain, and swelling. Bouvardia chrysantha has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menstrual cramps, fever, and digestive problems, thanks to its ability to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension. Bouvardia chrysantha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
